title: Systems and methods for provisioning accounts
abstract: Systems and methods are provided for use in provisioning accounts to applications included in mobile devices. One exemplary method includes receiving, at a mobile device, a request to provision an account to a mobile device; prompting a user associated with the account for authentication at a wireless device associated with the account; receiving an account credential from the wireless device, via a local wireless communication between the mobile device and the wireless device, when the user is authenticated at the wireless device; transmitting the account credential toward a first party associated with the account, whereby the account credential is indicative of the authentication of the user; and provisioning the account to the mobile device, in response to an approval received from the first party.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A computer- implemented method for use in provisioning an account to a mobile device, the method comprising: receiving, at a mobile device, a request to provision an account to a mobile device; prompting a user associated with the account for authentication at a wireless device associated with the account, wherein the wireless device is different than the mobile device; receiving, at the mobile device, an account credential from the wireless device, via a local wireless communication between the mobile device and the wireless device, when the user is authenticated at the wireless device; transmitting the account credential toward a first party associated with the account, whereby the account credential is indicative of the authentication of the user at the wireless device; and provisioning the account to the mobile device, in response to an approval received from the first party. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein transmitting the account credential toward the first party includes encrypting the account credential, based on an encryption key associated with a network provider, and transmitting the encrypted account credential to the network provider, thereby permitting the network provider to transmit the account credential to the first party. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising detecting a tap of the wireless device at the mobile device and enabling wireless communication with the wireless device, in response to the detected tap. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 3, wherein the wireless device includes a payment card; and wherein receiving an account credential from the wireless device includes receiving a payment account credential from the wireless device, in response to the detected tap of the wireless device at the mobile device. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising enabling the local wireless communication with the wireless device in response to the received request, thereby powering the wireless device. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 6, further comprising activating a biometric sensor of the wireless device in response to enabling the local wireless communication. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein provisioning the account to the mobile device includes receiving a token lfom a network provider and storing the token in memory of the mobile device, thereby permitting the mobile device to provide the token to a merchant in connection with an account transaction. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving, by the mobile device, an authentication result from the wireless device; and transmitting the authentication result toward the first party along with the account credential. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the mobile device includes a payment application, and wherein provisioning the account to the mobile device includes provisioning the account to the payment application; and wherein the wireless device includes a biometric enabled contactless payment card. 10. A system for use in provisioning a payment account to a payment application of a mobile device, the system comprising: a mobile device including a memory, a network interface, and a processor coupled to the memory and the network interface, the processor configured to: identify a tap from a payment card; receive an authentication result and a payment account credential from the payment card in response to the tap, via wireless communication with the payment card, through the network interface; 

 transmit the authentication result and the payment account credential to a network provider, thereby permitting the network provider to request approval to provision a payment account associated with the payment account credential to the a payment application of the mobile device; and receive and store a token associated with the payment account in the memory, whereby the payment account is provisioned to the mobile device. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the network interface is configured for near-field communication (NFC); and wherein the processor is configured to receive the payment account credential via a NFC connection with the payment card. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the processor is configured to enable the network interface for NFC connection with the payment card in response to the tap, thereby powering the payment card via the NFC connection. 13. The system of claim 10, wherein the processor is further configured to encrypt the authentication result and the payment account credential; and wherein the processor is configured, in connection with transmitting the authentication result and the payment account credential, to transmit the encrypted authentication result and the encrypted payment account credential. 14. The system of claim 10, further comprising the payment card having a memory and a biometric sensor coupled to the memory; and wherein the payment card is configured to: capture a fingerprint of the user at the biometric sensor; compare the captured fingerprint to a reference biometric stored in the memory of the payment card; and transmit the authentication result, based on the comparison, to the mobile device. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein the payment card further includes a power supply. 16. The system of claim 10, further comprising a computing device associated with the network provider, said computing device configured to: receive the authentication result and the payment account credential from the mobile device; determine if the payment account is within a range of payment accounts, based at least in part on the received payment account credential; and transmit the authentication result and the payment account credential to an issuing party associated with the payment account, when the payment account is within the range of payment accounts. 17. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium including computer-executable instructions for use in provisioning a payment account to a payment application of a mobile device, which, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: identify a tap from a payment card associated with a payment account; receive, from the payment card, via wireless communication with the payment card, an authentication result for a user associated with the payment card and a payment account credential for the payment account associated with the payment card; transmit the authentication result and the payment account credential to a network provider, thereby permitting the network provider to request approval to provision the payment account associated with the payment account credential to the a payment application of the mobile device; and receive and store a token associated with the payment account in memory, whereby the payment account is provisioned to the mobile device based solely on the wireless communication with the payment card and the authentication result for the user. 18. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the executable instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to cause a biometric sensor associated with the payment card to activate in response to the tap from the payment card. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the executable instructions, when executed by the processor in connection with transmitting the authentication result and the payment account credential to a network provider, cause the processor to transmit the authentication result and the payment account credential to a network provider without storing the payment account credential in the memory.
